<!-- Generated by tools/host-lisp/v11_l_lite_keymap.py. Do not edit. -->
# Workbench key bindings

The 1.1 L-lite profile uses the MEGA65 GETIN-compatible key codes. It does not
claim physical Meta/Alt modifier identity; the command launcher is `C-x x` or
`C-x Return`. `C-x Space` sets the mark because code zero means an empty queue
and therefore cannot represent C-Space on this input path.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Return` | Insert a newline |
| `Delete` | Delete the preceding character |
| `Cursor Left` | Move left |
| `Cursor Right` | Move right |
| `Cursor Up` | Move up one line |
| `Cursor Down` | Move down one line |
| `C-d` | Delete the following character |
| `C-f` | Move right |
| `C-b` | Move left |
| `C-s` | Search forward |
| `C-l` | Move to a line number |
| `C-n` | Move down one line |
| `C-p` | Move up one line |
| `C-j` | Insert a newline |
| `C-a` | Move to the start of the line |
| `C-e` | Move to the end of the line |
| `C-o` | Move forward one word |
| `C-u` | Move backward one word |
| `C-w` | Kill the following word |
| `C-r` | Kill the preceding word |
| `C-v` | Move down one page |
| `C-z` | Move up one page |
| `C-k` | Kill to the end of the line |
| `C-y` | Insert the latest killed text |
| `C-x C-s` | Save the current buffer |
| `C-x C-f` | Open a file |
| `C-x C-w` | Write using a prompted name |
| `C-x C-b` | Switch to a named buffer |
| `C-x C-d` | Open the source directory |
| `C-x C-k` | Compile and load the current buffer |
| `C-x C-n` | Select the next buffer |
| `C-x C-p` | Select the previous buffer |
| `C-x C-a` | Move to the start of the buffer |
| `C-x C-e` | Move to the end of the buffer |
| `C-x C-r` | Kill the marked region |
| `C-x C-x` | Exchange point and mark |
| `C-x C-y` | Copy the marked region |
| `C-x Space` | Set the region mark |
| `C-x C-c` | Return to the REPL and preserve buffers |
| `C-x x` | Open the command launcher |
| `C-x Return` | Open the command launcher |

RUN/STOP is not an editor key. During evaluation it aborts to a usable REPL
with `stopped (run/stop)`; while idle it has no product action. Exit the editor
with `C-x C-c`; the active buffer is preserved.

## Command launcher

Command names are matched exactly; two-character prefixes are not accepted.

| Command | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `find-file` | Open a file |
| `save-buffer` | Save the current buffer |
| `compile-load` | Compile and load the current buffer |
| `goto-line` | Move to a line number |
| `eval-buffer` | Evaluate the current buffer |
